libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Configure generated tests to pass --add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED to the JVM test task and to native-image build arguments, matching the existing 2.1.7 scaffold. Prefer public com.google.inject APIs for coverage; if tests intentionally cover internals, note that 2.9.1 jarjar-shades many internal classes with leading-$ Java class names such as com.google.inject.internal.util.$MapMaker and com.google.inject.internal.cglib.proxy.$MethodProxy, so unprefixed internal imports from older tests will not compile.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8858 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.sonatype.sisu:sisu-guice:2.9.1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: The current javac failure is not evidence of a missing dependency; the unprefixed internal classes are absent from the resolved 2.9.1 artifact.
Old shaded CGLIB code fails at runtime on modern modular JDKs without opening java.lang because it reflectively accesses ClassLoader#defineClass.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency or Docker service is needed: sisu-guice 2.9.1 is a self-contained old Guice fork with javax.inject and aopalliance as normal transitive dependencies. Meaningful Guice injector coverage on modern JDKs does need module opening for its shaded CGLIB bytecode path.
